LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING -A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS AMERICAN INTERNATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA INCORPORATED, A I U INSURANCE COMPANY ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS A I G PRIVATE CLIENT GROUP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING. ASSUMING THAT THERE IS AN AVAILABLE REMEDY FOR BREACHING THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING BY UNREASONABLY WITHOLDING CONSENT TO A SETTLEMENT, THIS REQUIRES THE COURT TO DENY THE MOTION WITH RESPECT TO THE DUTY TO INDEMNIFY BECAUSE THAT CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATION, IF PLAINTIFF COULD PROVE IT, WOULD BE AN ISSUE THAT WOULD PROVE A BREACH OF CONTRACT. THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO BREACH OF CONTRACT FOR FAILURE TO INDEMNIFY IS DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GRANTED. PER DIEM FEES ASSESSED BY THE COURT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). 301/PJB

The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Number 52. Any documents identified shall be subject to a confidentiality order.
The motion is granted as to Request For Production No. 10 to AIU and AIIC and Number 11 to AIIC. Any documents produced shall be subject to a confidentiality order. Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege shall be identified with particularity.
Further responses and production shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
